text.title: Tailwind Design Tokens Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Tailwind Design Tokens. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: tailwind.config.js (or v4 CSS config) is GENERATED from tokens.json; manually edited Tailwind theme keys are rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual Tailwind edits create a second source; CI must rebuild the config from tokens on every change.

rule.statement: Tailwind theme.colors / theme.spacing / theme.fontSize keys mirror the SEMANTIC tier of tokens (e.g. theme.colors.feedback.error), not the global tier.
rule.rationale: Mirroring global produces value-named utilities (bg-red-500) instead of intent-named (bg-error-500); semantic mirror keeps utilities tied to design intent.

rule.statement: Arbitrary value syntax (bg-[#FF6633], p-[12px]) is CI-blocked in production code; arbitrary syntax is allowed only in marked prototype files.
rule.rationale: Arbitrary values are escape hatches that erode the token layer; the CI block makes drift visible.

rule.statement: On semantic token rename, Tailwind safelist + purge config are updated to avoid orphan classes shipping to production.
rule.rationale: Without purge update, renamed tokens leave dead classes shipping bytes to users.

rule.statement: Dark mode uses class strategy (darkMode: 'class') with semantic tokens that swap values; media-query strategy is rejected for token-driven projects.
rule.rationale: Class strategy lets users override system preference; media-query strategy ties theming to the OS without override.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-tailwind-design-tokens.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
